_notes.env.ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH: force deferred MCP tool loading; auto mode unreliable
_notes.autoMemoryEnabled: disable auto MEMORY.md writes; CLAUDE.md still active
_notes.permissions.defaultMode: bypassPermissions launches in skip-prompts mode WITHOUT the --dangerously-skip-permissions CLI flag. Set here (not via shell alias) because cmux's shell integration unaliases `claude` and wraps it as a function on the first prompt (cmux-zsh-integration.zsh _cmux_fix_path), which strips an alias-injected flag. Still honors the ask/deny rules below (e.g. git commit still prompts, git push/secret reads still denied).
_notes.permissions.deny Read(**/.env.*): blunt: blocks the Read tool for ALL env variants incl scaffolds (.env.example). No per-file exception is possible — eval order is deny>ask>allow, first match wins, and Read patterns have no gitignore negation. Read scaffolds via `cat .env.example`; secret-guard.sh allows scaffold cats while still blocking real .env reads.
_notes.secret-guard: PreToolUse hook (Bash+Grep) keeping secret material out of context; pairs with these deny rules. Regression suite: claude/hooks/secret-guard.test.sh
_notes.permissions git * commit/push: Bash matching is literal (not shell-aware re: git global flags), so `Bash(git commit:*)` only catches commands literally starting with `git commit` — `git -C <path> commit`, `git -c k=v commit`, `git --git-dir=… commit` bypass it. The mid-wildcard twin `Bash(git * commit:*)` covers those flag forms (the `*` spans intervening args); the plain `Bash(git commit:*)` is kept because `git * commit` won't match the no-flag form. Same pair guards the `git push` deny.
